response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned individual contributor with broad technical depth and genuine developer-tooling experience (test runners, CI/CD infrastructure, MCP frameworks), but the role is fundamentally a people-management position requiring at least 2 years of formal team leadership. The resume shows no evidence of direct reports, performance reviews, hiring, or org-level planning—the core activities of a Level II Engineering Manager. His Staff titles appear to be IC seniority markers rather than management designations, and while his workshop facilitation and mentorship instincts are visible, they do not substitute for managing engineers. Without that management foundation, the minimum bar for the role is not met.
strengths: Genuine developer-tooling pedigree: authored a test runner (Trial), an assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD infrastructure at Altom, and MCP code generators at re:cinq—directly relevant to the team's focus area
Exceptional technical breadth across web, mobile (iOS/Apple TV), embedded, low-latency systems, and cloud-native stacks, which would help a manager empathize with and review diverse engineer work
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing culture: organized recurring Refactoring Dojos, Code Katas, and multi-module Selenium courses, signaling coaching instincts useful for team development
concerns: Zero people-management experience: no evidence of direct reports, performance cycles, headcount decisions, or formal team leadership in any role listed
No strategic or roadmap ownership: every position describes execution-level IC work; there is no indication of setting team priorities, influencing org-level goals, or leading organizational change
Mobile-platform depth is thin and dated: the iOS/Apple TV work was a one-person interim effort in 2015–2016, far below the platform-ecosystem management expertise the role targets
